_meta.review_notes: Heuristic auto-review 2026-04-22: verified source URLs match a .gov/official domain where possible, rejected items matching industry-convention/proposed-not-enacted/unverified-rate patterns. Remaining entries require secondary admin approval before draft->active flip.

notes: 1. The INGUAT 10% hotel tax predates modern records and has been in force since at least the late 1960s under Decreto 1701 (Ley Orgánica del INGUAT); its exact original effective date is approximated. The reglamento was last significantly amended via Acuerdo Gubernativo of July 22, 1980. 2. The GTQ 2.00/day minimum threshold for the INGUAT hotel tax is from the 1969/1980 legislation and is effectively irrelevant in practice today given current room rates, but is technically still part of the legal framework. 3. The Antigua Guatemala municipal fee (Acta 075-2022) was subject to legal/constitutional debate at the time of introduction (July 2022) — critics argued municipalities cannot levy taxes without Congressional approval — but no court order suspending or repealing the fee was found. The fee appears to remain in force. 4. The sat.gob.gt priority domain provided general tax administration information and hosted the Decreto 27-92 IVA text, but did not contain specific accommodation tax rate pages directly. The INGUAT official site (inguat.gob.gt) provided the most authoritative source on the hotel occupancy tax. 5. All rates marked as 'new' since there are no previously recorded rates for GT-ANT in the database. All rates are currently in effect.

rates.legal_reference: Decreto 1701 (Ley Orgánica del INGUAT); Acuerdo Gubernativo M. de E. 33-69, Artículo 26
rates.source_quote: EL INGUAT, percibirá un impuesto del diez por ciento (10%) por hospedaje que se cobrará sobre las tarifas, excluida alimentación y otros servicios, en hoteles, móteles, campamentos, pensiones y demás centros de alojamiento, cuando la tarifa autorizada sea de dos quetzales o más por día o fracción diaria.

rates.legal_reference: Decreto 27-92 (Ley del Impuesto al Valor Agregado), Artículo 10
rates.source_quote: Hablando en términos generales, el Impuesto al Valor Agregado (IVA) en Guatemala es un impuesto sobre el consumo con una tasa general del 12%.

rates.legal_reference: Acta 075-2022, Reglamento para el cobro de tasa municipal por hospedajes, Artículo 4
rates.source_quote: La normativa establece que todo servicio de alojamiento turístico deberá de cobrar a los huéspedes, por cada habitación ocupada, una tasa de Q20 diarios, hasta un máximo de cinco noches.

rates.legal_reference: Decreto 27-92 (Ley del IVA), Régimen de Pequeño Contribuyente
rates.source_quote: Si usted utiliza este régimen, no estará obligado al pago del Impuesto Sobre la Renta y sus servicios estarán sujetos a una tarifa reducida de IVA del 5%.

rules.description: The INGUAT 10% hotel occupancy tax does not apply to stays of 30 consecutive days or more (monthly stays). Hotels providing accommodation on a monthly or 30-consecutive-day basis are exempt from collecting or remitting the 10% INGUAT tax for those stays.
rules.legal_reference: Acuerdo Gubernativo M. de E. 33-69, Artículo 26; Decreto 1701 Ley Orgánica INGUAT
rules.name: Long-Stay INGUAT Tax Exemption (≥30 Days)
rules.source_quote: El impuesto establecido a favor del INGUAT se aplicará al usuario y no estará involucrado en las tarifas que sobre hospedaje apruebe el INGUAT; deberá hacerse público y no se cobrará cuando se trate de hospedaje en habitaciones o apartamentos por periodos mensuales o de treinta días consecutivos.

rules.description: The Antigua Guatemala municipal flat fee of Q20 per occupied room per night is capped at a maximum of 5 nights per stay. No additional municipal fee is charged for nights beyond the fifth night of a continuous stay.
rules.legal_reference: Acta 075-2022, Municipalidad de Antigua Guatemala, Artículo 4
rules.name: Antigua Municipal Fee 5-Night Cap
rules.source_quote: De acuerdo con el documento todo servicio de alojamiento turístico de Antigua Guatemala deberá de cobrar a los huéspedes, por cada habitación ocupada, una tasa de Q20.00 diarios, hasta un máximo de cinco noches.

rules.description: The Q20 per night municipal lodging fee in Antigua Guatemala applies only to accommodation establishments classified as 4-star, 5-star, or boutique hotels. Lower-category establishments (1–3 star hotels, hostels, guesthouses) are not subject to this municipal fee.
rules.legal_reference: Acta 075-2022, Municipalidad de Antigua Guatemala, Reglamento Artículo 10
rules.name: Antigua Municipal Fee – 4/5-Star and Boutique Hotels Only
rules.source_quote: la tasa para incentivar actividades culturales, de arte y de recreación para el turismo en la Antigua Guatemala, es aplicable solamente a los hoteles categorizados como cuatro y cinco estrellas y hoteles boutique.

rules.description: Accommodation providers who are resident in Guatemala and whose annual income does not exceed GTQ 60,000 may register under the Small Taxpayer (Pequeño Contribuyente) regime, which subjects their services to a reduced 5% VAT rate instead of the standard 12%, but disqualifies them from deducting input VAT credits.
rules.legal_reference: Decreto 27-92 (Ley del IVA), Régimen de Pequeño Contribuyente
rules.name: Small Taxpayer Reduced VAT Eligibility
rules.source_quote: Usted puede aplicar a este régimen si es residente en Guatemala y sus ingresos anuales no superan GTQ.60.000.

rules.description: The INGUAT 10% hotel tax only applies to accommodation where the authorized daily tariff is 2 quetzales or more per day (or fraction thereof). Establishments charging below this threshold are not subject to the INGUAT hotel tax.
rules.legal_reference: Acuerdo Gubernativo M. de E. 33-69, Artículo 26; Decreto 1701
rules.name: INGUAT Tax Minimum Rate Threshold
rules.source_quote: EL INGUAT, percibirá un impuesto del diez por ciento (10%) por hospedaje que se cobrará sobre las tarifas, excluida alimentación y otros servicios, en hoteles, móteles, campamentos, pensiones y demás centros de alojamiento, cuando la tarifa autorizada sea de dos quetzales o más por día o fracción diaria.

summary: Research identified 3 distinct accommodation-related taxes applicable in Antigua Guatemala: (1) A national 10% INGUAT hotel occupancy tax levied on the authorized room rate (excl. food/other services), established under Decreto 1701 (Ley Orgánica del INGUAT) and its Reglamento (Acuerdo Gubernativo M. de E. 33-69); (2) Guatemala's standard 12% VAT (IVA) under Decreto 27-92 applicable to all accommodation services; and (3) A municipal lodging fee (tasa municipal) of Q20 per occupied room per night, capped at 5 nights, applicable exclusively to 4- and 5-star hotels and boutique hotels in Antigua Guatemala, enacted via Acta 075-2022 and effective 30 July 2022. All three taxes are currently in force with no evidence of repeal or modification. A long-stay exemption (≥30 consecutive days) applies to the INGUAT 10% tax. A reduced 5% VAT rate is available for qualifying small taxpayers (annual income ≤ GTQ 60,000). 6 sources checked in total.
